meta.dataset: Child care gap: children potentially needing care vs. formal slots available
meta.source: Child Care Gaps Assessment — Bipartisan Policy Center, Buffett Early Childhood Institute (Univ. of Nebraska), Child Care Aware of America
notes: Covers children under age 5. 'Potential need' = children whose family/work circumstances suggest need for care; 'supply' = slots at legally operating providers (center- and home-based, incl. Head Start, Tribal, DoD). Distance-based methodology: a slot counts only if within reasonable driving distance of the child's home.
State, county, and congressional-district level data available in the interactive tool at childcaregap.org.

TODO: extract per-state need/supply/gap figures from childcaregap.org (interactive JS tool — check for a downloadable dataset or data-request option; BPC also publishes state fact pages). Verify terms of use for embedding state figures with attribution; as an advocacy-oriented public resource this is likely permitted, but confirm before launch.

CONVERSION TO JOBS: gap in slots → workers needed via state staffing ratios. National average ~36 children under five per childcare industry employee (Playground 2026 analysis), but regulatory child:staff ratios for care settings are much lower (e.g., ~4:1 infants, ~10:1 preschool). Use licensing-ratio-based conversion, cited per state or via a national blended ratio as v1 simplification.
